text:
# Work Order & Incident Report — DOC1205
**Document type:** Work Order / Incident Narrative (synthetic narrative record)
**Failure:** F1147 — Power Failure
**Equipment:** EQ-RMS-02 — Roughing Mill Stand R2 (Rajendra Steel Plant, Mumbai Unit - Rolling Mill)
**Event timestamp:** 2026-03-29 14:32:50  |  **RCA:** RCA1147 (closed 2026-04-03)

## 1. Incident Narrative
At 14:32:50 on 2026-03-29, the Roughing Mill Stand R2 raised a power failure alarm and was taken out of normal operation. Condition monitoring captured air temperature 302.5 K, process temperature 313.9 K, rotational speed 1864 rpm, torque 64.8 Nm and accumulated tool wear 34 minutes at the moment of the trip. The shift crew isolated the equipment under the plant permit-to-work system and raised this work order for investigation and repair.

## 2. Response & Diagnosis
Ravi Singh (Shift C), Anjali Desai (Shift A) attended the equipment and completed the make-safe checks before handover to the investigation team. The root cause analysis RCA1147, performed by Swati Desai (equipment engineer, Shift A), concluded: Root cause was a power-delivery failure: the product of rotational speed and torque pushed process power outside the acceptable window, indicating a drive/supply anomaly. An unstable supply and drive fault were the primary contributors.

## 3. Procedure Review
The investigation reviewed the governing procedure PROC-024 for this asset and recorded a compliance finding: Step 3 of PROC-024 ("Isolate power and apply lockout-tagout, then bleed hydraulic and pneumatic pressure to zero.") — the electrical isolation/calibration check was missed in the prior PM cycle. This finding is the actionable link between the maintenance routine and the failure mechanism, and it was logged so recurrence checks can compare like-for-like across the fleet.

## 4. Corrective Work Completed
Inspected and repaired the drive, verified supply stability, and added power-envelope monitoring with an automatic trip. Post-repair verification confirmed return to the qualified operating envelope before release to production. Quality review linked 12 failed quality test(s) across coils C10031, C10137, C10174, C10318, C10333, C10349… produced on this equipment in the surrounding production window; the associated deviations were referenced back to this work order for disposition tracking.

## 5. Closure
Work order closed on 2026-04-03 with the corrective action verified in service. This record is a synthetic narrative generated for the Synapse knowledge graph and tagged accordingly. Linked records: failure F1147, RCA RCA1147, procedure PROC-024, equipment EQ-RMS-02.
